title: abies bifolia var. arizonica
intro: Abies bifolia var. arizonica, also known as the Arizona fir, is a variety of fir tree that is native to the southwestern United States. It is a type of coniferous tree that is rich in essential oils, particularly terpenes, which have been used for their medicinal properties. The bark and needles of the Arizona fir have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the essential oils of the Arizona fir can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can help to relieve coughs, soothe sore throats, and promote respiratory health.
